88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). iter-187 supplies the missing
96	predicate as a project-side definition via local trivialisation on an affine
97	open cover (see `IsLocallyTrivial` below), and refines `OnProduct` to the
98	corresponding subtype.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- A sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules `M` is **locally trivial of rank one** if every
104	point `x : X` has an affine open neighbourhood `U` on which the restriction
105	`M|_U` is isomorphic to the structure sheaf-as-module `𝒪_U`. This is the
106	project-side stand-in for the missing Mathlib `IsInvertible` predicate on
107	`Scheme.Modules`: line bundles (invertible sheaves) are exactly the
108	`IsLocallyTrivial` modules, by Stacks tag 01HK / Hartshorne II §6. Local
109	trivialisability is the structural content that survives base change along
110	`f : Y ⟶ X` (Stacks 01HH) and is the source of the invertibility-preservation
111	step for `pullbackAlongProjection`.
112	
113	The witness is bundled as `Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit _)`:
114	the existence of a sheaf-of-modules isomorphism over the affine chart. -/
115	def IsLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
116	  ∀ x : X, ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
117	    Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf)
118	
119	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
120	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
121	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
122	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
123	Picard functor at `T`.
124	
125	iter-187: refined to the subtype of `(Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules` cut out
126	by the project-side predicate `IsLocallyTrivial` (locally trivial of rank one).
127	The carrier is now genuinely the line-bundle subobject of `Scheme.Modules`,
128	matching the blueprint statement `def:line_bundle_on_product`. Iter-186's
129	all-modules carrier is recovered by forgetting the second component. -/
130	def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=
131	  { M : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }
132	
133	/-- Projection from a line bundle on the product to its underlying
134	sheaf of modules. -/
135	abbrev OnProduct.carrier {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
136	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules := L.1
137	
138	/-- A line bundle on the product is locally trivial of rank one. -/
139	lemma OnProduct.isLocallyTrivial {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
140	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : IsLocallyTrivial L.carrier := L.2
141	
142	/-- **Stacks 01HH (pullback of an invertible sheaf is invertible).** For any
143	morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and any locally-trivial sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules
144	`M`, the pulled-back module `f^* M : Y.Modules` is again locally trivial.
145	
146	The proof unfolds an affine open cover trivialising `M`, pulls back along `f`,
147	and observes that `f^*` commutes with restriction along open immersions (the
148	`pullback`/`restrictFunctor` natural isomorphism in
149	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) so that the trivialising chart for
150	`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
151	involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
152	preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
153	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
154	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
155	(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
156	lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
157	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
158	    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
159	  intro y
160	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
161	  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
162	  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
163	  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
164	  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
165	  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
166	  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
167	  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
168	  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
169	  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
170	  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM
171	  set g : (V : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := f.resLE U V hVU with hg_def
172	  have hg_comp : g ≫ U.ι = V.ι ≫ f := Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι f hVU
173	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final :=
174	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
175	  refine ⟨?_⟩
176	  -- 1: ((pullback f).obj M).restrict V.ι ≅ (pullback V.ι).obj ((pullback f).obj M)
177	  let i1 :=
178	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback V.ι).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
179	  -- 2: ≅ (pullback (V.ι ≫ f)).obj M
180	  let i2 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp V.ι f).app M
181	  -- 3: ≅ (pullback (g ≫ U.ι)).obj M  (using V.ι ≫ f = g ≫ U.ι)
182	  let i3 := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hg_comp.symm).app M
183	  -- 4: ≅ (pullback g).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
184	  let i4 := ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g U.ι).symm).app M
185	  -- 5: ≅ (pullback g).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
186	  let i5 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso
187	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M)
188	  -- 6: ≅ (pullback g).obj (unit_U)
189	  let i6 := (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapIso eM
190	  -- 7: ≅ unit_V  (pullback of unit is unit via the Mathlib morphism, which is
191	  -- iso since the underlying `Opens.map g.base` is Final)
192	  let i7 := asIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom)
193	  exact i1 ≪≫ i2 ≪≫ i3 ≪≫ i4 ≪≫ i5 ≪≫ i6 ≪≫ i7
194	
195	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
196	
197	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
198	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
199	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
200	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
201	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
202	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
203	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
204	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
205	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
208	01HH). -/
209	
210	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
211	curve. Sends a *line bundle on `T`* — i.e. an `O_T`-module `N` equipped with a
212	proof `hN : IsLocallyTrivial N` — to the line bundle `π_T^* N` on `C ×_S T`,
213	induced by the `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on `Scheme.Modules` together
214	with the preservation step `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH).
215	
216	iter-187: refined to consume the `OnProduct`-style subtype on the source: the
217	underlying carrier is the direct application of Mathlib's
218	`Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)` functor at `N`, and the
219	locally-trivial witness comes from the project-side helper
220	`IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` (Stacks 01HH, named typed sorry pending the affine
221	chart chase). -/
222	noncomputable def pullbackAlongProjection {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
223	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (N : T.Modules) (hN : IsLocallyTrivial N) :
224	    OnProduct πC πT :=
225	  ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N,
226	    hN.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)⟩
227	
228	/-! ## §3. Composition of pullbacks
229	
230	The composition law for line-bundle pullback (Stacks tag 01HG) says that for any
231	composable morphisms `f : X ⟶ Y, g : Y ⟶ Z` and any line bundle `M ∈ Pic(Z)`,
232	the natural isomorphism `(g ∘ f)^* M ≅ f^* g^* M` makes the pullback functor
233	into a (pseudo-)functor `Sch^op ⥤ Cat`.
234	
235	Specialised to the relative curve setting: given `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the
236	base-change morphism `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` satisfies
237	`π_T ∘ g_C = g ∘ π_{T'}` (definition of the fiber product). Therefore on
238	line bundles `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*`, with the equality realised as the
239	canonical natural isomorphism of pullback functors via the pseudo-functoriality
240	of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (Mathlib `Modules.pullbackComp`).
241	
242	Blueprint reference: `lem:pullback_compose` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
243	
244	/-- **Composition of line-bundle pullbacks** along the projection. For a base
245	scheme `S`, a curve-side morphism `πC : C ⟶ S`, test objects `T, T'` over `S`
246	via `πT : T ⟶ S` and `πT' : T' ⟶ S`, and a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`
247	(encoded by the hypothesis `πT' = g ≫ πT`), set
248	`g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T`
249	(the base-change morphism, given by `pullback.map`). On the underlying
250	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` functors the two routes through the canonical
251	naturality square give canonically isomorphic objects:
252	```
253	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)
254	  T.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T).Modules
255	       │                                                              │
256	       │ Scheme.Modules.pullback g            Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C
257	       ▼                                                              ▼
258	 T'.Modules  ─────────────────────────────────────────────►  (C ×_S T').Modules
259	            Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')
260	```
261	i.e. for any `N : T.Modules`,
262	```
263	(Modules.pullback g_C).obj ((Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N)
264	  ≅
265	(Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT')).obj ((Modules.pullback g).obj N).
266	```
267	
268	This natural isomorphism is the substantive content of the
269	"line-bundle pullback functor is well-behaved under composition" fact
270	(Stacks tag 01HG). At the level of the `OnProduct` carrier (which is a typed
271	`sorry` in iter-174; see §1) it descends to equality of isomorphism classes
272	`[g_C^* π_T^* N] = [π_{T'}^* g^* N] ∈ OnProduct πC πT'`.
273	
274	iter-186: closed via the chain
275	1. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (pullback.snd πC πT)` (functor iso applied at `N`)
276	2. `Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr` for the equation `g_C ≫ snd πC πT = snd πC πT' ≫ g`
277	   (`pullback.lift_snd` on the `pullback.map` lift).
278	3. `(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (pullback.snd πC πT') g).symm` (applied at `N`). -/
279	theorem pullback_pullback_eq {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
280	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T)
281	    (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) (N : T.Modules) :
282	    Nonempty
283	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
284	            (Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
285	              (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg]))).obj
286	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N) ≅
287	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT')).obj
288	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N)) := by
289	  set g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
290	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
291	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
292	  have key : g_C ≫ Limits.pullback.snd πC πT = Limits.pullback.snd πC πT' ≫ g :=
293	    Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
294	  refine ⟨?_⟩
295	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g_C (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT)).app N ≪≫ ?_
296	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr key).app N ≪≫ ?_
297	  exact ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (Limits.pullback.snd πC πT') g).app N).symm
298	
299	end LineBundle
300	
301	namespace RelPicPresheaf
302	
303	/-! ## §4. The relative Picard quotient (set-valued)
304	
305	The relative Picard presheaf `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`
306	quotients the line-bundle group of the product by the subgroup pulled back from
307	the base. As a quotient of abelian groups it is a quotient set; equivalently,
308	the equivalence relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` is captured by a
309	`Setoid` on the line-bundle type `OnProduct πC πT`. The quotient
310	`Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the underlying set of the relative
311	Picard presheaf at `T`.
312	
313	The "preimage_subgroup" name follows the Kleiman/Stacks convention: the
314	quotient is by the preimage `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)`, treated as a
315	subgroup (image of a group homomorphism); the project-side Lean encoding
316	extracts the *Setoid* that the quotient construction takes.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined` (Kleiman §2,
319	Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
320	
321	/-- **Well-definedness of the relative Picard quotient as a `Setoid`.**
322	
323	The equivalence relation on `OnProduct πC πT` defining the relative Picard
324	presheaf
325	```
326	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
327	```
328	is the relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`. This relation is reflexive
329	(`L ⊗ L⁻¹ = O_(C ×_S T) = π_T^* O_T ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`), symmetric (the inverse of
330	a line bundle in `π_T^* Pic(T)` is in `π_T^* Pic(T)`), and transitive
331	(`L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` and `L' ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)` give
332	`L ⊗ L''⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`); hence is an equivalence relation, encoded by a
333	`Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT`.
334	
335	The substantive content is the well-definedness of the quotient: the canonical
336	projection `OnProduct πC πT → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` is the
337	set-theoretic underlying map of the relative Picard presheaf at `T`.
338	
339	iter-186: with `OnProduct := (pullback πC πT).Modules`, the carrier is the
340	modules category. We encode the **iso-class setoid** `L ~ L' ↔ Nonempty (L ≅ L')`.
341	This is a substantive simplification: it identifies isomorphic modules
342	(giving the underlying set of `Pic(C ×_S T)` once invertibility is imposed),
343	but does not yet quotient by the pullback subgroup `π_T^* Pic(T)`. The full
344	quotient requires a tensor-product structure on `Scheme.Modules` (Mathlib
345	ships `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` but no monoidal structure on
346	`Scheme.Modules` directly at `b80f227`) plus an inverse for invertibles;
347	iter-187+: refine to `L ~ L' ↔ ∃ N : T.Modules, Nonempty (L ⊗ L'⁻¹ ≅
348	pullbackAlongProjection πC πT N)` once both are available. -/
349	noncomputable def preimage_subgroup {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
350	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
351	    Setoid (LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT) where
352	  r L L' := Nonempty (L.carrier ≅ L'.carrier)
353	  iseqv :=
354	    { refl := fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
355	      symm := fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩
356	      trans := fun ⟨e₁⟩ ⟨e₂⟩ => ⟨e₁ ≪≫ e₂⟩ }
357	
358	/-! ## §5. Naturality in the test scheme — the relative Picard presheaf as a functor
359	
360	For a morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S` (encoded by the equality `πT' = g ≫ πT`),
361	the base-change `g_C := id_C ×_S g : C ×_S T' ⟶ C ×_S T` induces a map
362	`g_C^* : Pic(C ×_S T) → Pic(C ×_S T')` on line bundles, which (by
363	`lem:pullback_compose` = `pullback_pullback_eq` above) sends the subgroup
364	`π_T^* Pic(T)` into `π_{T'}^* Pic(T')`. Therefore it descends to a unique map
365	on quotients
366	```
367	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T'),    [L] ↦ [g_C^* L],
368	```
369	making `Pic^♯_{C/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` a presheaf.
370	
371	For the iter-174 file-skeleton, we pin the **object-level** functorial action
372	(i.e. the induced map of quotient sets for a single test morphism `g`). The
373	full `Functor` packaging (with the identity / composition laws supplied as
374	`map_id`, `map_comp` fields) is iter-175+ work once `OnProduct` and the
375	`preimage_subgroup` carrier are unpacked.
376	
377	Blueprint reference: `thm:pullback_natural` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
378	functor" + Def. `df:Pfs`; Stacks tag 01HG). -/
379	
380	/-- **The relative Picard presheaf is functorial in the test scheme.**
381	
382	Object-level functorial action: for any morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, there
383	is a canonical map
384	```
385	g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) ⟶ Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')
386	```
387	of quotient sets, factoring `g_C^*` through `preimage_subgroup πC πT` and
388	`preimage_subgroup πC πT'`. On representatives, `g^♯ [L] = [g_C^* L]` where
389	`g_C := id_C ×_S g`. The factorisation through the quotient is well-defined
390	because `g_C^*` sends `π_T^* Pic(T) ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T)` into
391	`π_{T'}^* Pic(T') ⊆ Pic(C ×_S T')` (this is the commutative-square content of
392	`pullback_pullback_eq`).
393	
394	iter-186: with the iso-class setoid `preimage_subgroup`, `Quotient.lift`
395	on `L ↦ ⟦(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L⟧` is well-defined because
396	functors preserve isomorphisms. The identity / composition laws giving the
397	full `Functor (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` packaging follow from `pullbackId` /
398	`pullbackComp` and are iter-187+ work (they currently aren't needed by any
399	downstream consumer). -/
400	noncomputable def functorial {S C T T' : Scheme.{u}}
401	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) (πT' : T' ⟶ S) (g : T' ⟶ T) (hg : πT' = g ≫ πT) :
402	    Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT) → Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT') :=
403	  let g_C : Limits.pullback πC πT' ⟶ Limits.pullback πC πT :=
404	    Limits.pullback.map πC πT' πC πT (𝟙 C) g (𝟙 S)
405	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]) (by rw [Category.comp_id, hg])
406	  Quotient.lift
407	    (fun L : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT =>
408	      Quotient.mk (preimage_subgroup πC πT')
409	        (⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).obj L.carrier,
410	          L.isLocallyTrivial.pullback g_C⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT'))
411	    (fun _ _ ⟨e⟩ => Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback g_C).mapIso e⟩)
412	
413	end RelPicPresheaf

I found the key insight: `tensorObj_restrict_iso` needed open immersions only because it routed through `pushforward`/`restrictScalars` (the *lax* coextension). But `pullback` = extension of scalars = the *left adjoint*, which is **strong monoidal for any ring map** (base change of tensor). So `IsInvertible.pullback` for general morphisms should be feasible via presheaf extension-of-scalars strong monoidality + the already-used `sheafificationCompPullback`. Let me verify Mathlib's infrastructure.

# blueprint-writer directive — iter-239, slug `invpull`

## Chapter to edit
`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` — ONLY this chapter.

## Goal
Add a new subsection blueprinting the **pullback-monoidality / invertibility-under-pullback substrate**
for general scheme morphisms. This is the prerequisite for re-basing the relative-Picard consumer
(`OnProduct`/RPF) onto the `IsInvertible` carrier (Route Y of A.1.c). Place it AFTER the existing
restriction-compatibility material (`lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`) and BEFORE / separate from the
group-law section `sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier` (do NOT modify the group-law section).

## Strategy context (the slice that matters)
The project's Picard group `picCommGroup` (DONE) is carried on tensor-invertibility
`IsInvertible M := ∃ N, Nonempty (M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X)` (Stacks 0B8K). The relative-Picard consumer needs to
re-base its line-bundle carrier `OnProduct` onto `IsInvertible`, and its functorial pullback maps
(`pullbackAlongProjection` along the projection `C×_S T → T`, `functorial` along the base-change
`g_C : C×_S T' → C×_S T`) require that **pullback preserves tensor-invertibility** for GENERAL scheme
morphisms (these are not open immersions, not flat).

IMPORTANT distinction to record in the prose: the existing `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso` handles only the
OPEN-IMMERSION case, and it does so by routing through `pushforward`/`restrictScalars` (the LAX right
adjoint, strong-monoidal there only because an open immersion's ring map is a local isomorphism). The
general case must instead use the LEFT adjoint — pullback = inverse image = extension of scalars — which
is **strong monoidal for ANY ring map** (no iso hypothesis). This is the whole reason the general lemma
is a separate result, not a corollary of the open-immersion one.

## Required new blocks (give each a `\lean{...}` pin and a precise mathematical proof sketch)

1. **`lem:pullback_tensor_iso`** — pin `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIso}`.
   Statement: for any morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M, N ∈ Scheme.Modules X`,
   `(Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) ≅ tensorObj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)
   ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj N)`, natural in `M, N`.
   `\uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}`.
   % SOURCE: Stacks tag for `lemma-tensor-product-pullback`, references/stacks-modules.tex lines 2392–2400.
   % SOURCE QUOTE: copy verbatim L2392–2400 (the statement `f^*(F⊗G) = f^*F ⊗ f^*G functorially`).
   Proof sketch (mirror the structure of `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso` but via the LEFT adjoint):
   - `tensorObj M N` is by definition the sheafification of the presheaf tensor
     `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val`.
   - The genuine Mathlib lemma `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` moves the pullback INSIDE the
     sheafification (it is general — holds for any ring-sheaf map, already used for the open-immersion
     case), reducing the goal to the presheaf level:
     `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (pullback M).val ⊗ₚ (pullback N).val`
     where `φ = f`'s ring-sheaf map and `⊗ₚ = PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`.
   - The presheaf-level pullback `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ` is STRONG monoidal: sectionwise it is
     extension of scalars, and Mathlib provides `(extendScalars f).Monoidal` (strong-monoidal extension
     of scalars for any ring hom, in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean`).
     The strong-monoidal tensorator `μ` of this functor furnishes the presheaf comparison; sheafifying
     it gives the claim.

2. **`lem:pullback_unit_iso`** — pin `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackUnitIso}`.
   Statement: for any `f : Y ⟶ X`, `(Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
   ≅ SheafOfModules.unit Y.ringCatSheaf` (i.e. `f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`).
   Proof sketch: the monoidal unit is preserved by the strong-monoidal pullback — the unit comparison
   `ε` of `(extendScalars f).Monoidal` is the iso `S ⊗_R R ≅ S`; at the sheaf level this descends to
   `f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`. Note: a Mathlib morphism `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` exists (it is used in
   `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`, where it is an iso for open immersions via finality); flag whether it is
   an iso for general `f` or whether the iso must come from the `extendScalars` unit comparison instead.

3. **`lem:isinvertible_pullback`** — pin `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible.pullback}`.
   Statement: for any `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M ∈ Scheme.Modules X`, `IsInvertible M →
   IsInvertible ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M)`.
   `\uses{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible, lem:pullback_tensor_iso, lem:pullback_unit_iso}`.
   % SOURCE: Stacks `lemma-pullback-invertible`, references/stacks-modules.tex lines 4142–4147 (statement)
   % and lines 4149–4157 (proof).
   % SOURCE QUOTE: copy verbatim L4142–4147.
   % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: copy verbatim L4149–4157.
   Proof sketch (the Stacks proof, in project notation): from `IsInvertible M` get a witness `N` with
   `e : M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`. The pullback witness is `(pullback f).obj N`; the required iso
   `(pullback f).obj M ⊗_Y (pullback f).obj N ≅ 𝒪_Y` is
   `(pullbackTensorIso).symm ≪≫ (pullback f).mapIso e ≪≫ pullbackUnitIso`.

## Verified Mathlib handles to cite in the prose as available ingredients (do NOT write Lean tactics)
- `(extendScalars f).Monoidal` — strong-monoidal extension of scalars, any ring hom [verified present
  this iter in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean`].
- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` — sheafification commutes with pullback [verified: already
  consumed by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`].
- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `PresheafOfModules.pullback`, `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`.

## Citation discipline
You are authorized `references/**`. Open `references/stacks-modules.tex` and copy the verbatim
`% SOURCE QUOTE` / `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF` text from the exact line ranges above. A visible
`\textit{Source: Stacks, ...}` line opens each block's prose. Do NOT cite from memory.

## Out of scope (do NOT touch)
- The group-law section (`sec:tensorobj_invertibility`, `sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier`) — already complete.
- The `OnProduct` re-base itself (that is a future `Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex` edit).
- Markers: do NOT add `\leanok` / `\mathlibok` anywhere (managed deterministically downstream).

iso-classes of **invertible** `𝒪_X`-modules under ⊗, where invertible means tensor-invertible:
`IsInvertible M := ∃ N, Nonempty (M ⊗ N ≅ 𝒪_X)` (Stacks 0B8M/01CX — the definition of an invertible
module; equivalent to locally-free-rank-1 on any ringed space). Carrying the group on THIS predicate
makes the group axioms near-trivial: identity `[𝒪_X]`; closure from associator+braiding; **inverse
`[M]⁻¹ := [N]` is the membership witness — free, no inverse object to construct** (well-defined since
`N` is iso-unique). `CommRing.Pic` (= `Skeleton (Module.Invertible R)`) is NOT reusable (it reads its
group off a coherent `MonoidalCategory` over a FIXED ring, unavailable for the varying `𝒪_X`), so the
`CommGroup` is built by hand; pentagon/coherence is never invoked. The restriction linchpin
`tensorObj_restrict_iso`, unitors, and braiding are axiom-clean. Carrying the group on invertibility
deletes the inverse-object manufacture (internal-hom dual / cocycle gluing) off the critical path
entirely — a strict reduction, the dual deleted not renamed.

`tensorObj_assoc_iso`. The d.2 ingredient (`stalkTensorIso`, the varying-ring stalk-tensor commutation

### `lem:pullback_tensor_iso` — `lemma-tensor-product-pullback`, L2392–2400

Blueprint `% SOURCE QUOTE`:
```
\begin{lemma}
\label{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}
Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be
a morphism of ringed spaces. Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$
be $\mathcal{O}_Y$-modules. Then
$f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_Y} \mathcal{G})
= f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} f^*\mathcal{G}$
functorially in $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$.
\end{lemma}
```

**Validation result: PASS** — byte-accurate match with `references/stacks-modules.tex` L2392–2400.

Blueprint `% SOURCE QUOTE` (statement):
```
\begin{lemma}
\label{lemma-pullback-invertible}
Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be a
morphism of ringed spaces. The pullback $f^*\mathcal{L}$ of an
invertible $\mathcal{O}_Y$-module is invertible.
\end{lemma}
```

Blueprint `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`:
```
\begin{proof}
By Lemma \ref{lemma-invertible}
there exists an $\mathcal{O}_Y$-module $\mathcal{N}$ such that
$\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_Y} \mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_Y$.
Pulling back we get
$f^*\mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} f^*\mathcal{N} \cong \mathcal{O}_X$
by Lemma \ref{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}.
Thus $f^*\mathcal{L}$ is invertible by Lemma \ref{lemma-invertible}.
\end{proof}
```

**Validation result: PASS** — both byte-accurate against `references/stacks-modules.tex` L4142–4147 and L4149–4157.

# Iter-239 — detailed objectives

## Lane 1 — `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` [mathlib-build]: `IsInvertible.pullback` substrate

**Why:** the group law `picCommGroup` landed iter-238 on the `IsInvertible` carrier. The next
critical-path step (A.1.c — author the relative Picard functor on `IsInvertible`) requires that
**pullback preserves tensor-invertibility** for GENERAL scheme morphisms (the projection
`C×_S T → T` and the base-change `g_C : C×_S T' → C×_S T` are neither open immersions nor flat). This
is the substrate prerequisite; without it the consumer cannot be authored on `IsInvertible`.

**Blueprint:** `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex`, new section `sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`
(`lem:pullback_tensor_iso`, `lem:pullback_unit_iso`, `lem:isinvertible_pullback`), written + cleaned this
iter; SOURCE = Stacks `lemma-tensor-product-pullback` / `lemma-pullback-invertible`
(references/stacks-modules.tex).

**Targets (bottom-up):**
1. `pullbackTensorIso` : `(Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) ≅ tensorObj ((pullback f).obj M)
   ((pullback f).obj N)` for any `f : Y ⟶ X`. Recipe — mirror `tensorObj_restrict_iso` but via the LEFT
   adjoint: (a) `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` [verified present] moves the pullback inside
   sheafification → presheaf-level goal; (b) the presheaf pullback is strong monoidal sectionwise via
   `(extendScalars f).Monoidal` [verified, `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean`];
   its tensorator `μ`, sheafified, gives the iso. Do NOT route through `pushforward`/`restrictScalars`
   (the lax right adjoint — that is the open-immersion-only `tensorObj_restrict_iso` route).
2. `pullbackUnitIso` : `(pullback f).obj (unit) ≅ unit` (`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`). From the `ε` unit comparison of
   `(extendScalars f).Monoidal` (`S ⊗_R R ≅ S`). OPEN sub-question handed to prover (blueprint flags it):
   is Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` an iso for general `f` (it is for open immersions
   via finality, used in `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`), or must the iso come from the `extendScalars` `ε`?
3. `IsInvertible.pullback` : `IsInvertible M → IsInvertible ((pullback f).obj M)`. Stacks proof: witness
   `(pullback f).obj N`; iso `pullbackTensorIso.symm ≪≫ (pullback f).mapIso e ≪≫ pullbackUnitIso`.

**mathlib-build invariant:** no sorry pins. Land 1–3 axiom-clean as far as possible; if `pullbackTensorIso`
needs presheaf-monoidal assembly that doesn't close in one iter, commit the unit iso + whatever presheaf
comparison lands and hand off a precise decomposition. progress-critic ts239 watch: if the first attempt
adds ≥3 helpers without closing `IsInvertible.pullback`, the next step is a mathlib-analogist consult on
`sheafificationCompPullback` carrier semantics — NOT another blind dispatch.

## Lane 2 — `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` [prove]: close `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`

**Status:** progress-critic ts239 = STUCK, but **one post-corrective attempt is justified** — the iter-238
blueprint expansion gave the prover the element-free `D(a)`-transport recipe it lacked (material new info,
not a verbatim re-dispatch).

**Target:** the sorry at L470 (`affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`). Build the unconditional
`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` by discharging `hloc` via the element-free `D(a)`-level transport
(`e_{D(a)}` linear equiv mirroring `gammaPushforwardIso` + the `D(a)` ring equation +
`IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv` / `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` transport of `M[1/φa]`),
then feed `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule` (L395, conditional, already axiom-clean) ⇒
unconditional brick ⇒ close `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`.
Blueprint: `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`, `lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (expanded last iter).

**Do NOT attempt** the L492 sorry `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` (deep Čech+flatness; documented).

**HARD reversing signal (progress-critic ts239):** if sorry stays at 2 after this iter's review, the next
corrective is a ROUTE PIVOT (a different decomposition of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` that does NOT
traverse `hloc` via `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv` — e.g. a direct scheme-level `IsQuasicoherent`
criterion), preceded by a mathlib-analogist consult. NOT a fourth blueprint expansion. The smul carrier
wall has now recurred at 3 section locations (`⊤` → Γ → `D(a)`); a 4th recurrence means the architectural
path itself is the problem.

## Deferred this iter
- RPF (`RelPicFunctor.lean`) re-base — gated on Lane 1 (`IsInvertible.pullback`) landing. Next iter:
  author `OnProduct`/`pullbackAlongProjection`/`functorial`/`PicSharp` on `IsInvertible` (in

- HigherDirectImage, CMRegularity/SemiContinuity chapters — gap-blocked (unchanged).

36	### Proposed chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_CMRegularity.tex`
37	
38	**Covers**: (new file, ~1 Lean file `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/CMRegularity.lean`)
39	**Strategy phase**: A.2.c-engine (Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity)
40	**Why now**: Writing the blueprint now (even if the chapter is non-dispatchable) pins the mathematical spec and lets the writer produce a complete outline that the prover can use the moment the higher-direct-image sub-lane opens.
41	
42	**Key declarations** (in dependency order):
43	1. `\definition` `\label{def:cm_regular}` — Castelnuovo–Mumford $m$-regularity of a coherent sheaf on a projective scheme. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsCMRegular}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §2 (`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`).
44	2. `\lemma` `\label{lem:cm_vanishing}` — $m$-regular ⟹ $H^i(\mathcal{F}(r)) = 0$ for $i ≥ 1$, $r ≥ m$. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CMRegular.higherCohomVanishing}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §2.
45	3. `\lemma` `\label{lem:cm_globalGeneration}` — $m$-regular ⟹ $\mathcal{F}(m)$ globally generated. Source: Nitsure §2.
46	4. `\theorem` `\label{thm:mumford_bound}` — Uniform CM-regularity bound: for fixed Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$ and ranks, there exists $m = m(n, p, \Phi)$ such that $E_s$, all its coherent quotients of Hilbert polynomial $\Phi$, and their kernels are $m$-regular. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.mumfordRegularityBound}` [expected]. Source: Nitsure §2, Theorem "Mumford".
47	
48	**`\uses` skeleton**:
49	- `thm:mumford_bound` uses `def:cm_regular`, `lem:cm_vanishing`, `lem:cm_globalGeneration`, `def:higher_direct_image`
50	- `lem:cm_vanishing` uses `def:cm_regular`, `def:higher_direct_image`
51	- `lem:cm_globalGeneration` uses `def:cm_regular`
52	
53	**Main theorem proof strategy**: The CM-regularity definition (vanishing of twists ≥ m) implies global generation via a Čech-complex argument on affine covers. The Mumford uniform bound is the key non-trivial result, using the Hilbert polynomial stratification and induction on dimension; the proof is in Nitsure §2. All steps depend on `def:higher_direct_image` being in place.

input.content:
# Iter-239 plan-agent run

## Headline outcome

The **"group law DONE → open the A.1.c substrate `IsInvertible.pullback`; FlatBaseChange gets its one
post-corrective attempt"** iter. iter-238 landed `picCommGroup` axiom-clean (the ~20-iter group-law
bottleneck CLOSED). The critical path pivots to A.1.c — authoring the relative Picard functor on the
`IsInvertible` carrier. Investigation this iter found the consumer re-base is NOT cheap: its pullback maps
(projection `C×_S T → T`, base-change `g_C`) need `IsInvertible.pullback` for GENERAL morphisms. That is
FEASIBLE via Mathlib `(extendScalars f).Monoidal` (strong-monoidal extension of scalars, verified) +
`sheafificationCompPullback` — Route Y, chosen over the deferred dual-gluing Route X. Two prover lanes
dispatched; the RPF functor re-base itself deferred to next iter (gated on the substrate).

## What I processed (iter-238 outcomes)
- **TensorObjSubstrate.lean: `picCommGroup` + 9 deps DONE** axiom-clean (the by-hand carrier-pivot Picard
  `CommGroup`; step-0 also made `tensorObj_assoc_iso` unconditional). → migrated to task_done; processed
  result cleared.
- **FlatBaseChange.lean: no prover iter-238** (STUCK corrective = blueprint expansion of the
  `D(a)`-transport). The prover re-engages this iter.
- **lean-vs-blueprint ts238 majors** (stale `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` title + IsLocallyTrivial-hypothesis
  note + stale `\uses`) — FIXED this iter (plan-agent blueprint edits).
- **blueprint-doctor finding** (malformed `\uses{\leanok …}` in FlatBaseChange) — FIXED (`\leanok`
  relocated to its own line).

## Investigation that drove the decision
- The RPF carrier `Quotient (preimage_subgroup πC πT)` sits on `OnProduct = {M // IsLocallyTrivial M}`
  (LineBundlePullback.lean). The new group `picCommGroup` is on `IsInvertible`. The inverse is free ONLY on
  `IsInvertible`; `IsLocallyTrivial` has no free inverse (that was the whole point of the carrier pivot).
- So an honest RPF group needs EITHER the `IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible` bridge (dual-gluing, deferred
  as hard) OR re-basing `OnProduct` onto `IsInvertible` — which forces `IsInvertible.pullback` (since
  `pullbackAlongProjection`/`functorial` currently use `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`).
- Probe: `tensorObj_restrict_iso` is open-immersion-only (routes through the lax `pushforward`/
  `restrictScalars`, strong only because an open immersion's ring map is a local iso). But pullback =
  inverse image = extension of scalars is **strong monoidal for ANY ring map** — Mathlib ships
  `(extendScalars f).Monoidal`. So `IsInvertible.pullback` for general morphisms is feasible, and matches
  the Stacks proof of `lemma-pullback-invertible` exactly. → Route Y is tractable; Route X (dual-gluing)
  is the harder deferred path. **Decision: Route Y.**

## Subagents dispatched
| Subagent | Slug | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| progress-critic | ts239 | **STUCK (FlatBaseChange — 1 post-corrective attempt justified, then route-pivot) / UNCLEAR (IsInvertible.pullback, fresh, backbone confirmed).** Dispatch-sanity OK. |
| strategy-critic | ts239 | **Route Y SOUND** (backbone lemmas verified). 2 must-fix: bridge reclassification (A.2.c) + STRATEGY.md format drift — both addressed. |
| blueprint-writer | invpull | **COMPLETE** — 3 pullback-monoidality blocks (`lem:pullback_tensor_iso/unit_iso/isinvertible_pullback`) + verbatim Stacks quotes. |
| blueprint-clean | ts239 | **PASS** — 1 Lean-leakage strip; all 3 source quotes byte-accurate. |
| blueprint-reviewer | ts239 | **BOTH active-lane chapters CLEAR HARD GATE** (must-fix NONE). |

## Decision made — dispatch the substrate + FlatBaseChange; defer the RPF functor re-base

**Chosen:** TWO prover lanes — (1) `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` [mathlib-build] = `IsInvertible.pullback`
+ its two component isos (the A.1.c substrate prerequisite); (2) `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` [prove] =
the ONE justified post-corrective attempt to close `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`. The RPF functor
re-base (LineBundlePullback + RelPicFunctor) is DEFERRED to next iter, gated on the substrate landing, and
needs a chapter-rewrite writer pass first.

- **Why the substrate now (not the RPF functor directly):** an RPF prover lane = one file, but the re-base
  spans LineBundlePullback.lean (OnProduct/pullbackAlongProjection) AND RelPicFunctor.lean
  (addCommGroup/functorial/PicSharp), and ALL of it depends on `IsInvertible.pullback`. Building the
  substrate first (bottom-up, USER directive #4) de-risks the whole re-base and keeps each lane to one file.
- **Why FlatBaseChange gets the attempt despite STUCK:** progress-critic ts239 explicitly sanctions exactly
  ONE post-corrective dispatch — the iter-238 blueprint expansion gave the prover the element-free `D(a)`
  recipe it lacked (material new info, not a verbatim re-dispatch). The reversing signal is armed: a 4th
  sorry-flat iter triggers a route pivot, NOT a 5th expansion.
- **LOC/risk:** substrate ≈ 3 isos, backbone Mathlib-verified, Stacks-matched — UNCLEAR but clean (watch
  the ≥3-helper churn marker). FlatBaseChange ≈ the `hloc` discharge via a known recipe.
- **Cheapest reversing signals:** (substrate) ≥3 helpers w/o closing → mathlib-analogist consult on
  `sheafificationCompPullback` carriers; (FlatBaseChange) sorry stays 2 → route pivot.

## Strategy-critic must-fixes — how addressed
1. **Bridge reclassification:** the `IsInvertible ⟺ IsLocallyTrivial` bridge was mislabeled "off critical
   path." Resolved: the FORWARD `IsInvertible ⟹ locally-free-rank-1` (coherence; Stacks
   `lemma-invertible-is-locally-free-rank-1`, the EASY direction) is now classified ON-critical-path at
   A.2.c (Quot embedding), ~1–2 iters, scheduled at A.2.c entry. The REVERSE dual-gluing stays genuinely
   off-path (never needed if the project carries `IsInvertible` throughout). STRATEGY.md + PROGRESS.md

## Iter-239 plan summary

**The "group law DONE → open the A.1.c substrate; FlatBaseChange gets its one post-corrective attempt" iter.**

iter-238 closed the ~20-iter Picard group-law bottleneck (`picCommGroup` axiom-clean). I processed that, then investigated the next critical-path step (A.1.c — the relative Picard functor on the `IsInvertible` carrier) and found the re-base is **not cheap**: its pullback maps need `IsInvertible.pullback` for general morphisms. Crucially, this is **feasible** — pullback = extension of scalars is strong monoidal for any ring map (Mathlib `(extendScalars f).Monoidal`, verified), matching the Stacks proof of `lemma-pullback-invertible` — unlike the deferred dual-gluing bridge. So **Route Y** (re-base onto `IsInvertible`) is chosen over Route X.

**Two prover lanes dispatched (both gate-cleared):**
1. `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` [mathlib-build] — `IsInvertible.pullback` + its two component isos (the A.1.c substrate prerequisite). New blueprint section written + cleaned + gate-cleared this iter via the same-iter fast path.
2. `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` [prove] — the ONE justified post-corrective attempt to close `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (progress-critic sanctioned: the iter-238 expansion gave the prover the element-free `D(a)` recipe). **Reversing signal armed:** a 4th sorry-flat iter triggers a route pivot, not another expansion.

The RPF functor re-base itself is deferred to next iter (gated on the substrate; needs a chapter-rewrite first).
